title: Phemeranthus brevicaulis
taxonomy: Phemeranthus brevicaulis is classified within the family Montiaceae. It belongs to the order Caryophyllales, a group of flowering plants that also includes carnations and wild pinks. The family Montiaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the purslane family. Phemeranthus brevicaulis is further classified within the genus Phemeranthus, which is a small group of flowering plants native to North America. The genus Phemeranthus is a relatively recent classification, having been separated from the genus Claytonia in the past.
uses: Phemeranthus brevicaulis, also known as sand penngil, is a plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its medicinal uses are not well-documented, but it has been studied for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Phemeranthus brevicaulis, also known as annual five-stamen or narrow-leaved globularia, is a perennial flowering plant species native to North America.
